Here Come The Girls EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 11-2 from 9-1 for her debut at Kempton (7f, AW) a fortnight ago, when she kept on well for a promising third; today's extra yardage seems a likely positive and she's a key player.

RaceName: Here Come The Girls EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: No match for the impressive winner when last of three at 6-1 on this month's debut at Hamilton (6f, good), and she could do with settling better over this longer trip; however, she still showed some promise in that warm little maiden and is open to improvement for last year's winning stable.

RaceName: Here Come The Girls EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Built upon previous promise to get off the mark at the fourth attempt at Pontefract (6f, good) recently; carries a 4lb penalty against some unexposed rivals today and on breeding there are reservations regarding the step up in trip, but she has strong claims if she stays; wears a first-time hood.

RaceName: Here Come The Girls EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: She has a useful pedigree and is one to watch in the betting on this second start; however, she was 13l behind Fern Clyde when eighth of 11 at 9-1 on her recent debut at Pontefract (6f, good) and needs to be an entirely different proposition this afternoon.

RaceName: Beverley Racecourse Supporting Racing Staff Week Handicap
Spotlight: Doubled his tally when winning at Musselburgh (7f, good) in April off 2lb lower; raced too close to the pace next time but has been placed in each of his last three starts, including at Catterick (7f, good to soft) 12 days ago; chance.

RaceName: Beverley Racecourse Supporting Racing Staff Week Handicap
Spotlight: Four of his five wins have been here over 8.5f (good to soft/good to firm), the most recent last month off 4lb lower, and he was runner-up to This Farh over C&D next time; well held at Chester (7.5f, soft) 11 days ago but it would be no surprise to see him bounce back returned to this track.

RaceName: Beverley Racecourse Supporting Racing Staff Week Handicap
Spotlight: Has been in tremendous form this year, winning five of his last six starts, the most recent under Rhys Elliott at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) eight days ago; unpenalised for that success and holds good claims in hat-trick bid.

RaceName: Beverley Racecourse Supporting Racing Staff Week Handicap
Spotlight: Four of his five wins have been at this track (two over C&D); returned to form following a couple of lesser efforts when fourth at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) eight days ago but he was behind Roaring Ralph on that occasion.

RaceName: Beverley Racecourse Supporting Racing Staff Week Handicap
Spotlight: Often front-runner who won at Leicester (7f, good) in April and over this C&D (good to firm) last month; bit below his best at Chepstow 17 days ago but soft ground may have been against him (also lost a shoe).

RaceName: Beverley Racecourse Supporting Racing Staff Week Handicap
Spotlight: 550,000euros breeze-up 2yo who failed to build on his debut effort when an 11.5l third of 11 in a Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) in March; still looked in need of the experience in a novice at Nottingham (8.5f, good) 45 days ago but may do better now handicapping, albeit this opening mark looks stiff.

RaceName: Big Racing Weekender Next 3 And 4 July Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden but she's been in the first four on seven of those occasions, including in a handicap at Newmarket (1m, good) 39 days ago; may prove vulnerable back in maiden company but has place claims.

RaceName: Big Racing Weekender Next 3 And 4 July Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Sent off 150-1 on her debut at Southwell (1m, AW) last month but she showed plenty, finishing second of 11; looked green on that occasion, hanging left under pressure, and she's open to improvement now switched to turf; contender.

RaceName: Big Racing Weekender Next 3 And 4 July Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Well beaten when fifth on her debut at Newmarket (7f, good to soft) last summer but showed more at Hamilton (1m1f, good to firm) 27 days ago; comes from a nice family and she's open to further progress.

RaceName: Big Racing Weekender Next 3 And 4 July Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Promising second at Thirsk (1m, good) on his debut but he hung right on quick ground at Hamilton (1m1f) next time; respectable effort when fourth of 12 at Carlisle (1m1f, good to soft) two weeks ago but the likely quicker ground would be a slight concern.

RaceName: Big Racing Weekender Next 3 And 4 July Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Gelding by Mondialiste; first foal; dam unraced sister to US 5.5f/6f winner Faithful Ruler, half-sister to 10.5f winner Shamwari, out of 6f winning sister to 1m Group 2 winner Viscount Nelson and half-sister to 2yo Group 1 winners Horatio Nelson (7f) and Van Gogh (1m); market should be informative.

RaceName: Big Racing Weekender Next 3 And 4 July Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: 100-1 on his debut at Kempton (1m, AW) in November but shaped with plenty of promise, finishing third of 13; well beaten at Doncaster on his reappearance but it was more encouraging here over 1m2f two months ago, finishing third behind two subsequent winners; drops in trip with claims.

Seven-race maiden but she was beaten only 3l when fourth of 11 at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last time on her first start for 272 days; may come on for that and her pedigree suggests this longer trip is worth exploring.

Won a maiden hurdle at Musselburgh in November; did his best work late when runner-up at Redcar (1m6f, good) last month but he's lacked the pace to challenge in two starts since over this trip; visor goes back on for cheekpieces.

OwnerName: S Thompson And Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Dual winner for Charlie Johnston in 2024; runner-up over this C&D last May on his second start for this yard but he's lost his way since, albeit it was his first start for 301 days when beating only one rival home at Newcastle three weeks ago.

Ten-race maiden but she ran one of her better races when second of seven (beaten 3l) here over 8.5f 27 days ago; doesn't appear to be crying out for this step up in trip but she has claims if seeing it out.
Six of his seven wins have been on the AW at Southwell, the most recent by a wide margin over 1m6f in March under Elle-May Croot; held in four starts since and others are preferred.
21-race maiden and she beat only rival home here over 1m2f in first-time blinkers a week ago; steps back up in trip in a switch of headgear and others have stronger claims.

Made a winning reappearance at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm; made virtually all) in April but she's been well beaten in two starts since, including over this trip at Leicester 15 days ago; has a bit to prove.
Gained his third success when winning over this C&D last month off 3lb lower but wasn't at his best at York (1m2f, good) 11 days ago; unexposed on good to firm but needs to bounce back.
All three of his wins have been on ground slower than good but he does act on quick ground; went close at Ripon (1m2f, good) five days ago and steps back up in grade slightly with good claims.

Five wins from 39 starts, the most recent coming at York (1m2f, good) last June off a 6lb higher mark than today's; well beaten in two starts since returning from his second wind operation and now has cheekpieces applied.

Won a Redcar novice (1m2f, good to soft) in October when trained by Ralph Beckett; yet to match that level for this yard but did run well in a first-time tongue-tie at Chester (1m4f, good to soft) ten days ago, albeit aided by the run of things; unraced on ground quicker than good.

RaceName: Racing TV Extra On racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Achieved best finish yet when fourth of seven, beaten just over 6l, on recent handicap debut at Leicester (1m2f, good) where he went off 11-2; improvement is necessary today but that can't be ruled out.

RaceName: Racing TV Extra On racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: 0-5 but this front-runner hasn't been beaten far at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) in her two handicaps, finishing second of nine then fourth of four; this race possibly won't take much winning; firmly in calculations.

RaceName: Racing TV Extra On racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has been underwhelming in handicaps; however, her best RPR came when third in a 7.4f novice last September on her sole previous visit to Beverley, and it's possible going beyond 1m for the first time might suit this Time Test filly; not ruled out.

RaceName: Racing TV Extra On racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Well beaten at 80-1 or bigger on all three starts (7f-1m1f); however, she goes handicapping off a modest mark and her dam was useful; not discounted, and the betting could be informative.

RaceName: Racing TV Extra On racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: He's achieved little on his three starts (7f-8.3f), but there's potential in his pedigree and he makes his handicap debut off a lowly mark over a new trip and in first-time blinkers; from an in-form stable and he'll be all the more interesting if strong in the betting.

RaceName: Racing TV Extra On racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Very early days for this half-brother to three winners, who may have needed last month's handicap/seasonal debut at Redcar (1m, good); however, he was tailed off at 14-1 and has to leave that run miles behind.

RaceName: Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: She's raced six times and best effort yet when fifth of eight at 33-1 at Ripon (5f, good) last Wednesday, but another step forward is necessary.

RaceName: Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Only fourth in handicaps at Redcar and Carlisle on last two starts and needs something extra today if he's to get off the mark at the 12th attempt; however, he went close in a C&D seller (good to firm) last month and perhaps the return to Beverley will prompt a bigger performance.

RaceName: Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown hints of promise among her six (winless) starts and she suffered costly interference leaving the stalls at Doncaster recently; things may click at some point but this first attempt at 5f isn't guaranteed to suit and others arrive with more compelling claims.

RaceName: Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: She's dropping down the weights but her form has gone the wrong way in handicaps and she's 0-7; needs to turn things around.

RaceName: Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Took a step forward when second of six on recent handicap debut at Catterick (5f, good to soft), albeit no match for the odds-on winner; trainer is a dab hand with sprinters and she's open to further improvement.

RaceName: Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark on her eighth start at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last month and another good run when third at Ripon (5f, good) last Wednesday, despite an early stumble; solid contender.

RaceName: South Wales Medical Event Cover Handicap
Spotlight: Infrequent winner these days but always prominent when holding on at Chepstow (6f, good) four days ago; this looks tougher under a 4lb penalty (Alexandra Egan rides again).

RaceName: South Wales Medical Event Cover Handicap
Spotlight: Unraced on ground easier than good; beaten favourite in all his three handicaps but there were encouraging signs, especially with a view to dropping back to sprinting; considered.

RaceName: South Wales Medical Event Cover Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent and probably a career-best to win after a break at Chepstow (5f, good to firm; fine on softer); only 2lb higher so has to be taken seriously.

RaceName: South Wales Medical Event Cover Handicap
Spotlight: Inconsistent on the AW this year, aside from winning in February and without a win on turf since 2023; his recent third at Lingfield (soft) was quite remote.

RaceName: South Wales Medical Event Cover Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple winner who made all at Salisbury last month (6f, good) and was ridden differently when never involved at Catterick; had no penalty last time and now 5lb higher, but if returned to positive tactics the ground won't be an excuse.

RaceName: South Wales Medical Event Cover Handicap
Spotlight: Heavy-ground winner as 2yo so conditions will be fine and he won on the AW in April; not a lot to shout about since but he was highly tried last time.

RaceName: South Wales Medical Event Cover Handicap
Spotlight: Had a busy year but holding his form well and nothing wrong with his narrow defeat on good to soft at Windsor last time; solid claims.

RaceName: Connolly's Red Mills 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Broke slowly but finished well when 4l behind on good to soft at Catterick recently; that was his second run after a lengthy break; has won on soft.

RaceName: Connolly's Red Mills 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Missed 2025 and hasn't kicked on from an encouraging return on the AW, last time finishing tailed off on soft.

RaceName: Connolly's Red Mills 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner who is 0-6 on grass after finishing fifth of seven at Newbury (5f); also unraced on slow ground, she has enough to prove.

RaceName: Connolly's Red Mills 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
OwnerName: Homecroft Wealth, Paul J Dixon & Partners
Spotlight: Front-runner; 0-12 on turf but has placed form and he's a three-time AW winner; last run was disappointing (stays 6f) but had been running well prior to that; a greater concern would be the forecast slow ground.

RaceName: Connolly's Red Mills 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Won two 6f handicaps on good ground last summer when with Hugo Palmer; quiet in two runs for current yard (cheekpieces then a visor) and soft ground might not be what he wants.

Cost 260,000gns as a yearling; a closer when fourth at Newbury (7f, good) where he was only 2l off subsequent high-profile winner Extremely Zain and then pushed a previous Juddmonte-owned winner close at Doncaster (good to firm); ground this soft will be a first but he's the one to beat on form.
Wootton Bassett filly; ended up well held on last September's debut at Doncaster (7f, good), but didn't appear to be unfancied at 5-1 and she's no doubt capable of better; market informative.

Bred to stay at least 1m; positives to take from both her runs, last time coming from behind to be only 2l behind a promising Ralph Beckett horse at Carlisle (1m, soft); proven on the ground, she's seriously considered.

Stepped up on Newbury debut with a promising second at Goodwood (1m, good) and the two he divided have run well since; he was keeping on that day and is back in trip here, albeit on slower ground; every chance if coping with this surface.
Beaten about 6l in 1m/7f maidens and he'll need more here to threaten a breakthrough.
Beaten double-digit lengths in 6f maidens at Salisbury and Newbury; probably one for handicaps.
A lowly rated maiden over jumps who has shown nothing in two Flat novices (1m2f).
Half-sister to 5.7f/7f winner North View (RPR 88); dam unraced half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Fallen For You; useful pedigree but likely best watched on this belated debut.
100-1 when tailed off in this new headgear combinaton at Bath (extended 5f); no appeal.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: Uncompetitive in five races at two and no better on comeback run when tried in a hood; hard to warm to.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: Had a favourable track position when scoring at Chepstow (1m, soft) but came up shy back there off this 2lb higher mark when ridden by Charlie Tucker; only 2-34 but he should at least give his running.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: Easy winner off 5lb lower over C&D last August (good) and runner-up three times since then; however, below par both runs this term and slower ground could be troublesome.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: Only win and best form has been at sprint distances and yet to achieve anything of note beyond 6f.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: Yet again he missed the break before going down narrowly at Chepstow last week (1m, good; handles softer); too risky when he's almost certain to give himself another uphill battle by starting slowly.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: Having his 105th career start and the last of his eight wins was in August 2023; beaten 4.5l into third at Bath last time and others appeal more.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish winner; hold-up performer who finished well for second behind Scarfo off his much-reduced mark at Chepstow (1m, soft); needs a strong pace and he has major claims if he gets one.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: Only win came in March 2024 when trained in Ireland; has lost his way this year and finished last on stable debut despite going off favourite.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: He's gone very close in an AW handicap (in March) and should be okay on this slow ground as a son of Mayson; considered.

RaceName: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap
Spotlight: Poor so far and was tailed off on his handicap debut (1m, good) when tried in blinkers.
Bumper winner who appreciated the drop in class when doing down by only a neck in one of these at Lingfield (1m2f, good); clear claims if handling the slow ground.
Regressive maiden but hasn't been running too badly and it was a classified race in which he was second two starts back (8.6f); should run well in new blinkers.
Had eight races but only two for this yard; from 10lb out of the weights she hit the frame in a 1m2f handicap at Bath and definite claims with a repeat of that.
0-11 in Ireland; ran okay to be midfield in a 1m2f handicap at Bath on yard debut, well enough to consider at this level.
Can win a race and the ground was soft when beaten a shade under 4l in a 1m handicap at Chepstow most recently (first-time tongue-tie); thereabouts.
Fairly uncompetitive in all his nine races, in this headgear combination in last two starts; needs to find something from somewhere and there are doubts about the slow ground.
Has 3.5l to find with Pureis King on their Lingfield clash but her consistency ticks a big box at this level.

Showed precious little last season at 7f/1m; only 13-2 for her handicap debut at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) where she was reportedly unsuited by that track's undulations; could well leave that run behind and cheekpieces are tried.
This is easier than her three novice races (1m-1m3f) but she has a lot to prove nonetheless.
Well beaten in maidens and again in a classified race on the AW 21 days ago; was 50-1 that day in a first-time visor; now goes in blinkers.
7-32 on the AW and 2-19 on turf, but he has performed well on grass in his last two starts, latterly over 1m2f at Yarmouth where he didn't get the clearest of runs; respected.
Losing run is mounting up but on a good mark and placed in his last two races (1m2f); goes on all ground; hard to rule out.

OwnerName: Spiers & Hartwell Ltd & Mrs E Evans
Spotlight: Effective on good to soft; most recent AW form is not overly inspiring but on a perfectly good mark and can go well when fresh.
On a losing run but finished well to be close up in 1m2f/1m handicaps at Leicester (good to firm); she can cope with good to soft and should run well.
Her neck defeat on good to soft at Bath (1m) represents useful form for the level; wasn't as good at Yarmouth but possibilities if forgiven that; stays 1m2f.
Loves it here and loves the mud, so this dry spell has done him no favours; probably one for another day.
Beaten less than 10l in the German Derby last year; nowhere near that form for Marco Botti or Jamie Osborne but getting back on slower ground could help.
Won at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) and followed up from the front at Wolverhampton (9.4f), albeit narrowly; however, ground slower than good is looking an issue.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: In the frame in both starts last month, the latest when second of nine at Bath (5.7f, firm; 6-4); form of that race not really working out, though, so a bit more will be needed.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 12-1 when beaten half a length into second of nine (clear of the third) on her Chepstow debut (5f, soft) 17 days ago; likely to face very different conditions here, but still high on list while the extra furlong should suit on breeding.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Overcame market weakness when making a successful debut from a previous winner in a six-runner Wetherby novice (5.5f, good to firm) 19 days ago; her Group 2 entry next weekend suggested she is well-regarded and respected despite the penalty.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: February foal by Mohaather; first foal; dam 7f/1m winner (including AW; RPR 69), half-sister to winners Zain Sarinda (Listed-placed 9.4f) and Indian Sounds (5f-7f including 2yo), out of German 1m2f Listed winner; worth a market check on debut for stable that has won this race (or a division of it) three times with newcomers since 2016.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: March foal by Magna Grecia; half-sister to winners Welcome Sight (French 6.5f/9.5f including AW; RPR 82) and Super Mirage (1m 2yo; 73); dam unraced sister to useful 6f winner City Girl, out of 6f Listed winner, is granddaughter of top sprinter Lochsong; stable doesn't have many winning 2yo newcomers.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Sent off 16-1 and ran green when a well-beaten eighth of nine on her Chepstow debut (5f, soft) 17 days ago; should have come on for the experience, but she has around 13l to find with Big Hitter.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: March foal by Showcasing; 70,000gns foal, 40,000gns yearling; half-sister to 6f/8.5f winner Countess Clara (including 2yo/Jersey; RPR 76); dam 7f AW winner (68), closely related to smart Hong Kong 6f-1m winner Rewarding Hero, out of useful 5f 2yo winner; stable can get them ready first time and Oisin Murphy an interesting booking, being 12-48 (25%) for the yard in last five seasons.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Sent off 28-1 when beaten a long way into ninth of ten on her Windsor debut (5f, good to firm) this month; stable won this with a twice-raced filly two years ago.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
OwnerName: Mps Racing And Jjg Racing Partnership
Spotlight: 33-1 when a tailed-off last of five on her Kempton debut (6f, AW) last month.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: March foal by Sergei Prokofiev; half-sister to 6f AW winner Dark Sorceress (RPR 59); dam Group-placed 5f-6.5f 2yo winner (99), half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Dark Illusion, is granddaughter of sister to French 1m2f/10.5f Group 3 winner Trumbaka; stable does have winning 2yo newcomers and worth a market check.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Ran her best race so far when fourth of nine at Bath (5.7f, firm) four weeks ago, but was still beaten 6l (runner-up Arabica Queen runs in the first division); needs to find more for the cheekpieces.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Did best of the newcomers when beaten around 6l into third of eight behind a nice filly on her Goodwood debut (6f, soft; 10-1) 11 days ago; a player if coming on at all from that.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Both starts at Bath in April, showing ability on her debut (5f, good) but taking a step backwards (5.7f, firm) ten days later; nurseries may provide her with better opportunities.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: April foal by Cotai Glory; 36,000euros yearling; resold 50,000euros; dam 5f-1m winner (including 2yo; RPR 96), sister to 1m Group 1 winner Champers Elysees and 6f 2yo winner Elzem, out of maiden half-sister to Italian 1m Group 3 winner She Bat; stable doesn't have many winning 2yo newcomers, but still worth monitoring in the market.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 25-1 when seventh of ten on her Lingfield debut (6f, AW) 19 days ago; needs a big step forward.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 33-1 when eighth of ten on her Kempton debut (6f, AW) last month; improvement needed, albeit the stable won this with a once-raced filly in 2023.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 12-1 when a well-beaten fifth of six on her Chepstow debut (6f, good to soft) eight days ago; improvement required.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Sent off 25-1 and showed signs of greenness on her Chepstow debut (5f, soft) 17 days ago, but she kept on to finish a never-nearer fourth of nine (runner-up Big Hitter runs in the first division); dam's side of pedigree suggests the extra furlong should suit and respected for stable with a fine record in this race.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 50-1 when a well-held seventh of 11 on her Windsor debut (6f, good to soft) 15 days ago; much more needed; stable won this race two years ago.

RaceName: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Well held in both starts including when around 4l behind Greek Symphony at Goodwood debut 11 days ago; hood given a go, but she looks more of a nursery type.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 11-1 and led when neck second of six on recent debut at Doncaster (6.5f, good to firm), coming clear with odds-on newcomer; that was a promising start and she's bred to be suited by today's extra yardage; strong claims.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 150,000euros yearling who has a useful pedigree and was favourite when winning on last month's debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW); did it by a head and improvement is probably needed if she's to defy a penalty today, but she defied her evident inexperience last time and is a likeable prospect.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 20,000euros foal, £60,000 yearling; fourth foal; Sioux Nation half-sister to winners So Chic (7f/1m AW; RPR 69) and Diego Spirit (Italian 6f 2yo/1m); dam maiden from useful family; it's possible she'll need this debut outing but a market move would be interesting.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: J F Dean, Mrs J Scott & Lady Trenchard
Spotlight: There's potential in her pedigree but she was tailed off at 80-1 on debut here (6.5f, good to firm) this month and may be best watched for now.

RaceName: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: By Kingman; first foal; dam 1m-2m winner (including 2yo/Listed), half-sister to two winners out of useful 7f 2yo/1m4f winner; likely to improve for this debut run but no surprise if she goes well; watch the betting.
Ran well in all four starts late last year, including landing a big-field Lingfield novice (7f, AW); will need to hit the ground running in order to be competitive here after 195 days off, though.

OwnerName: Kevin Frost Racing Club & Mike Humphries
Spotlight: Admirable teenager who has a better strike-rate on the AW, but he has won over this C&D; finished a long way behind Signcastle City at York (7f, good) last month and probably best watched.

7lb below his last winning mark and didn't run badly when fourth of seven on his third start for the yard at Salisbury (7f, good) nine days ago, but he gave plenty of trouble beforehand; others preferred.

Promising when third of ten behind Touleen on her debut over C&D (good to firm) last August and has finished runner-up in two small-field contests over 6f/7f on the AW since returning in March; return to these conditions may help on handicap debut with blinkers given a go; interesting.

Holds his form well despite a busy schedule and appeared to appreciate the drop in trip when beaten half a length into second of 22 at York (7f, good) last time; up 2lb but this is a slightly weaker race; respected.
Course winner who is likely to have her ideal ground conditions, but still 3lb above her last winning mark and another furlong may have been preferable.

Responded well to the fitting of blinkers when beating three rivals in a Yarmouth novice (6f, soft) last month; has another furlong and probably different ground to contend with, but that latest form has since been franked by the runner-up; high on list on handicap debut.
Rated 7lb lower than on the AW, but is hard to warm to on his latest efforts; needs the first-time blinkers to make a difference.
Two wins at Newcastle (6f, AW) this year, but not very consistent and was well beaten at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) last month; 2lb out of the weights; stable also runs White Ladder.
Wasn't winning out of turn when scoring by a neck from 13 rivals at Salisbury (7f, good) last month; second and seventh horses have won since so shortlisted from 3lb higher.
Unplaced in five starts over this trip last year; has shown ability, but will do well to make a winning return in such a competitive contest after 259 days off.

Showed some ability in three starts on the AW during the winter and the return to this trip may help, but improvement will be needed on turf/handicap debut after four months off; has been gelded since last seen.

Returned to form when keeping on for fifth at Sandown (5f, good) recently and he's on a handy mark on last year's best 6f form, including a near-miss over C&D (good to firm); firmly in calculations back up in trip.

Reappeared with a promising third at Kempton (7f, AW) last month and he's 2lb lower than when winning at Windsor (6f, good) last August; he has a good record at Windsor but can also be effective elsewhere; chance.
Three wins last year and he's below his last winning mark; reappeared with fairly encouraging third at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) last month but he's failed to shine on his three outings since.

Hasn't made an impact this year in his first three handicaps but he made late headway when 4l sixth of eight at Lingfield (5f, good) two starts ago, suggesting a return to 6f would be a good fit, and soft ground may have been against him at Salisbury (6f) subsequently; remains of interest.
He's on a competitive mark on last October's good AW form but he went off the boil subsequently and this is his first start since January; unproven on turf; needs to get back on track.
Two turf wins, the latest a claimer at Leicester last October on final run for Eve Johnson Houghton; dropping down the weights but hasn't been shaping of late as though he's about to take advantage.

Won at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last December and was third there (7f) next time; absent since heavy defeat at Southwell (7f, AW) in February but he ran well last May following a break and should be fine back on turf and back down to 6f; not ruled out.

Runner-up in Lingfield novice (7f, AW) last December on third start; soundly beaten in 1m handicaps on her two starts this season; showed pace last time and it's possible this lightly raced 3yo will be suited by today's first crack at sprinting, but others have more compelling claims.

He's raced five times and is yet to finish better than fifth, but he's shown promise and wasn't beaten far in a handicap at Lingfield (7f, AW) in March on his sole run this year; hinted last time that the drop to 6f may suit; no surprise if he gives a good account.

Runner-up in Windsor novice (6f, good to firm) last August on her second 2yo start and she's of interest on that form; three lesser runs have followed but her two handicap starts have been on good to soft ground, which may not have been ideal; might leave recent comeback run miles behind.
Overcame trouble in running to win at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month and another good run when second under James Glennon at Goodwood (6f, good to soft) since; solid claims.

Turf maiden winner who showed spark as recently as January (AW) and he's dropped down the weights; however, he's struggled on grass on his last two starts and a major turnaround is needed in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Pump Technology Performance Under Pressure Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)
Spotlight: Has been a bit up and down since returning from a mammoth absence in April, but his second of five at Catterick (1m6f, good to soft) 12 days ago was handsomly franked by the winner at Newmarket on Saturday; has won over this far and a major player.

RaceName: Pump Technology Performance Under Pressure Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Comes into this off the back of winning a three-runner race over hurdles at Fontwell, but he is equally adept on the Flat with two wins in four-runner contests over C&D (good to firm) last summer, including this race off 1lb lower; respected.

RaceName: Pump Technology Performance Under Pressure Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Usually consistent, but her modest effort at Goodwood (2m, soft) last time was disappointing given she does handle any ground; needs to bounce back from that.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Bolted up under a positive ride to make it sixth-time lucky at Salisbury (1m2f, good) nine days ago; likely to go up a good deal more than her 5lb penalty, but whether her rivals will allow her as much rope this time is uncertain.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: Ms Caroline Instone & J S Moore
Spotlight: 8lb lower than when winning easily here (1m4f, good to firm) last July, but hasn't matched that form since and was a long last of four at Bath (11.7f, firm) six weeks ago; can be slowly away.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: P Afia, E Hobson, A Pegley, C&r Russell
Spotlight: Course winner who made a successful reappearance at Nottingham (1m2f, good) in April and again ran well to finish third of seven in a 0-90 back there last month (fourth and fifth won next time); up another 1lb, but still of serious interest back at this level and has won over this trip on the AW.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: 0-20 in handicaps and was behind Grey Fox when last of eight at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) this month; down the pecking order.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: In and out since winning at Leicester (1m4f, good) in April and was disappointing at Goodwood (1m6f, soft) 11 days ago given he has won over that far and in testing ground; has plenty of form on good to firm, but has a bit to prove; often misses the break.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Course winner who has dropped 2lb lower then last successful and has twice won off the back of an absence; however, he didn't show enough back from 313 days off at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) this month to be of immediate interest.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Was winning for the first time since his debut when just prevailing over 1m4f here (good) last month, but may not have been suited by the conditions when third of six at Yarmouth (11.5f, soft) 12 days ago; each-way claims.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Won a four-runner race at Salisbury (1m4f, good to firm) as a 3yo, but all five wins since have come on the AW; rated 9lb lower on grass but still hard to warm to on a rare turf appearance.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: 0-6; hasn't built on early promise, including three poor efforts on turf/dirt in Meydan, and little better in a Wolverhampton maiden (8.6f, AW) this month; only of interest if backed.

RaceName: Proper Job Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well beaten on last month's chasing debut but quickly got back in the groove on return to hurdling last week, winning readily by 7l at Stratford (2m2f, good; second run in cheekpieces); avoids a penalty and has obvious claims.

RaceName: Proper Job Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Useful in his prime but was largely disappointing last season, and suffered 46l defeat at Warwick (2m5f, good) on latest outing five weeks ago; drops in grade again and may benefit from the drop back in trip but needs to turn a corner.

RaceName: Proper Job Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ex-Irish 9yo with modest 1-28 strike-rate under both codes; absent since safely held in a chase in October; considered only if lively in the betting before this stable debut.

RaceName: Proper Job Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten under 10l when seventh in recent C&D (good) contest won by Cogital and continues to slip down the weights, but has not been firing on all cylinders in recent months and needs to up his game.

RaceName: Proper Job Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-13 over hurdles but bounced back from a poor run when close third of six over C&D (good) 11 days ago and remains on a good mark; no surprise if he's on the premises again.

RaceName: Proper Job Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Off since below-par run in October but the form of his breakthrough win at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) in September worked out quite well; cheekpieces (worn once last summer) are given another whirl; contender if fully tuned up.

RaceName: Proper Job Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Out of form over a range of trips for new stable this spring (five runs; including one on the Flat); drops back to 2m after 42l defeat over 2m7f in refitted cheekpieces (retained) ten days ago; revival needed.

RaceName: Celebrate Racing Staff Week Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fairly useful on the Flat at his best; unable to justify odds-on favouritism when second of six on recent hurdling debut at Hexham (2m, good; winner beaten since) but ought to have learned from that experience and might be better suited by this easier track.

RaceName: Celebrate Racing Staff Week Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won four times on the Flat when based in France and was respectable third of seven in Class 4 handicap on recent British debut (1m2f, soft; RPR 79); this will be the quickest turf she has encountered but she still commands respect on her hurdling debut.

RaceName: Celebrate Racing Staff Week Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to five Flat winners; dam placed 6f 2yo, sister to 2m Group 2 winner Illustrious Blue; has travelled from Cheshire to make his debut (stable's only runner on the card); market helpful.

RaceName: Happy Birthday June O'Dwyer Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Easily won four-runner maiden point on debut in April then brushed aside modest opposition in C&D maiden hurdle (good) on last month's rules debut, winning by 30l; meets a much tougher rival in Coumeenoole this time but it's very hard to gauge the limit of his ability; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Happy Birthday June O'Dwyer Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 0-22 in points and 0-17 under rules; placed a few times off lowly marks in handicap chases in 2023/24 and also in points this year but another minor role is probably his best hope.

RaceName: Happy Birthday June O'Dwyer Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: D Summersby J May W Dodd
Spotlight: Soundly beaten when 200-1 for run-of-the-mill Worcester maiden (2m, good) on recent hurdle debut; moves up in trip.

RaceName: Happy Birthday June O'Dwyer Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Struggled when a 200-1 outsider for first two hurdle runs, the latter over C&D three weeks ago.

RaceName: Happy Birthday June O'Dwyer Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Initially disappointing for new stable this year but easily landed gamble in Bangor handicap (2m7f, good) in March, and did not need to be at same level to justify skinny odds in Uttoxeter novice (2m7f, good) this month; has a big penalty but should still be hard to beat.

RaceName: Happy Birthday June O'Dwyer Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Completed for first time in five-race career when second of two finishers in three-runner maiden point last month but still can't be recommended on this hurdling debut.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Made inauspicious start to hurdling career, but kept on well for third here (2m1f, good) on handicap debut three weeks ago and today's move back up in trip looks the right move.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Went close at Exeter (2m7f, good) in April, but has very mixed record for current stable since the autumn; well beaten over 2m4f on latest outing and now 0-14 over hurdles; not the percentage call.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Battled on well to beat Saucats and open account over C&D (good) last month, his sixth start over hurdles; probably needs to improve again to overcome 5lb rise but that's possible.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Runner-up on consecutive C&D starts last year (latterly in this race) but absent since disappointing Flat run last August and returns with hurdle strike-rate standing at 0-17.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Seemingly aided by first-time cheekpieces when close second to Makinbecon over C&D (good) last month, but managed only fifth at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) recently and is now 0-23 over hurdles.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well beaten in three starts for new stable in early part of this year and also struggled when back on the Flat last month; makes handicap hurdle debut off a lowly mark and with cheekpieces refitted, but has quite a lot to prove.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles but returned to form with creditable second of ten at Worcester (2m, good) this month, after a break; remains on a good mark; major player if in similar mood again.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Refused to race on the Flat last month and again looked unenthusiastic on recent handicap hurdle debut here (2m1f, good), albeit he eventually made headway into sixth (behind Warfleet); remains a risky option.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles for Seamus Mullins but made the frame in two good-ground handicaps for him this spring (2m/2m1f); has refitted cheekpieces and Sean Bowen enlisted for this stable debut (same owner); interesting.

RaceName: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Unplaced all five hurdling starts in Ireland last season and pulled up in handicap here (2m1f, good) on recent stable debut; difficult to enthuse over.

Came good off lowly mark at Hereford (2m, good) in November and could plausibly have untapped potential over this longer trip, but made low-key reappearance when well-held fourth back at Hereford last month and others in this field have more pressing claims.

Won two three-runner points this spring, either side of close third in four-runner Warwick hunter chase (3m, good); has more on her plate on this handicap return/stable debut (same owner), but is well treated on some of last year's form and Sean Bowen is booked.

Won this race for Alison Thorpe a year ago (good ground; visored) and returned from layoff with close second for new stable at Southwell (3m, good; first-time blinkers) a fortnight ago; strong claims.

Needs to bounce back from last month's disappointing run at Cartmel, but was only beaten just over 3l when third over C&D (good to soft) on stable debut in April, despite clouting the penultimate fence; has possibilities if handling today's faster ground.

Rejoined Sophie Leech after remote second in selling hurdle last month; made no impact when 50-1 for 2m1f handicap hurdle three weeks later and reverts to chasing with quite a bit to prove; cheekpieces removed.

Lightly raced since winning 2m juvenile hurdle here in summer 2024; kept on for very respectable third of five over C&D (good) 11 days ago, and remains unexposed over this trip, but today's race is more competitive; swaps cheekpieces for first-time visor.

Came good in 16-runner handicap at Huntingdon (3m1f, good) in November, his eighth attempt, and returned from break with two good efforts this spring, latterly when third (just behind Jafimgoso) at Southwell (3m, good) three weeks ago; another to consider.
Tailed off on stable debut in April but placed three consecutive times since, most recently when rallying third of ten at Cartmel (3m1f, good) four weeks ago; probably won't be far away.

Bumper winner; took a while to get his act together over hurdles but showed good stamina to lead close home at Ludlow (2m5f, good) last month and was only beaten 1l when upped to 3m at Southwell (good) three weeks ago; ought to be in the thick of things again.
Showed the benefit of a break and his latest wind op when making all at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month; again ran well when close second of three over C&D (good) a fortnight later; respected.

OwnerName: S Clare G Kennington & C Knowles
Spotlight: Made all to exploit much-reduced mark over C&D in April and is only 3lb higher today but he's unreliable nowadays and was last of five at Plumpton (3m1f, good) on latest outing four weeks ago.

Multiple hurdle/chase winner; unseated rider in early stages of a chase last month, after a layoff, and was tailed-off 66-1 shot when back over hurdles at Worcester (2m7f, good) 17 days ago; others have much less to prove.

Six-time Flat winner; opened account over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good; refitted cheekpieces) last month, after a break, and ran at least as well when close/clear second over that C&D three weeks later; can make presence felt again over this longer trip.

Suffered dip in form after pair of 3m1f Hereford wins towards end of last season but seemed to run well when third of four at Fontwell (3m2f, good; headgear switched) a fortnight ago and is now 2lb below his last winning mark; enters calculations.

Four-time chase winner (once over C&D); soundly beaten over hurdles in February, after a long absence, and didn't achieve much when third of four at Fakenham (2m7f, good) in April; reverts to chasing on a good mark but her current ability is hard to gauge.

Well beaten all four hurdle/chase runs since returning from long absence for new stable last September but has slipped to a basement mark and might appreciate getting away from soft/heavy ground; dangerous to ignore.

Has modest 1-29 strike-rate under rules but the win was gained over C&D (last summer) and last month's reappearance run was much more encouraging than his eventual 78l defeat suggests; each-way claims at least.
Ended last season with three poor runs but is now 3lb lower than when making all over C&D (good) in cheekpieces just over a year ago and might be boosted by the returning headgear.

Returned from break with 36l defeat in April but last month's Fontwell fourth (3m2f, good) was more encouraging and the cheekpieces (worn for the second of last year's two chase wins) are now back on; won't mind if the ground is quicker than good; can't be ruled out.
Made the frame in two maiden points last month but faces tougher opposition in this first handicap chase since April 2023; fitted with first-time cheekpieces for stable debut; others look safer.

Not yet proven over this far but made the frame on final four outings last season (2m3f-2m4f, good) and was in front before penultimate-fence unseat at Fontwell (3m2f, good) last month; must be considered.

Low-mileage mare who won over hurdles here (2m5f, good) in April; managed only fourth over 2m7f at Bangor last month but sports first-time cheekpieces today and Paul Nicholls is evidently keen to move her up in trip again and switch her to fences.

RaceName: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 150,000gns foal, 320,000gns yearling; eighth foal; Havana Grey sister to two winners including Symbology (Group-placed 6f 2yo), closely related to 5f-7f winner El Caballo (including 2yo/Group 2), half-sister to two Italian 2yo winners; dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 91); bred to be a classy juvenile and she has to be of interest on debut.

RaceName: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mpr, A & Glo Outlaw & K Quinn
Spotlight: 1,000euros yearling, resold 25,000euros yearling, £70,000 2yo breeze-ups; third foal; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 69), half-sister to some talented winners notably Mac Love (7f-1m2f Group 3) and Club Oceanic (useful 1m-1m2f); this Inns Of Court newcomer could give a good account.

RaceName: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 90,000gns yearling, 215,000euros 2yo breeze-ups; eighth foal; No Nay Never brother to winners Noble Vow (5f/5.7f; RPR 81) and Angelica Bay (US 1m turf); dam 6.5f and 1m (Listed) 2yo winner; he's travelled from Newmarket to make his debut and could have a big part to play.

RaceName: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 7-2 when fifth of ten on debut at Nottingham (5f, good) three weeks ago, beaten just over 4l by the winner Senorita Bonita who was runner-up at Royal Ascot last week; this 305,000euros yearling has an attractive pedigree and is open to improvement for a top trainer who won this last year with one taking a big step forward on her second start.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By No Nay Never; third foal; dam unplaced, half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Midnight Mile, out of maiden close relative of Irish 1,000 Guineas winner Yesterday and Moyglare Stud winner Quarter Moon (both runner-up in Oaks); yard hasn't been firing on all cylinders with its juveniles but did have one run a screamer at Royal Ascot last week; betting can guide.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Stepped it up on all three starts over 5f, culminating with solid third of eight in the Hilary Needler at Beverley (good to firm) 25 days ago; that wasn't the strongest renewal of that race but 6f should be no problem and she sets a clear standard; the one to beat.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 110,000euros yearling by Blue Point; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Soldier's Empire (1m/1m2f including 2yo/AW/Bahrain; RPR 96), Mariesque (9.4f; 94), Green Soul (7f 2yo; 83) and Green Backspin (Scandinavian 6f-1m1f); dam French 7f (Listed) and 1m winner (99); respected yard among the winners and she needs monitoring in the market ahead of her debut.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 70,000gns yearling by Too Darn Hot; 8-1 when eighth of 12 at Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) on her introduction three weeks ago; that form's received a few boosts, however; tongue-tie goes on for her second start.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Bangkok; second foal; dam 6f winner (RPR 78), half-sister to winners Bear Faced (7f AW), Mercenary Rose (5f AW including 2yo) and Munificent (5f including AW), out of useful 6f-8.6f AW winner; not ruled out on pedigree but yard is 0-39 with juveniles over the last five years.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Make Believe colt; 40,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Princess Mia (1m; RPR 67) and Wishforluck (Swedish 6f/7f including 2yo); dam French 1m4f winner, half-sister to winners Secret Existence (10.6f) and Twenty Times (1m2f), out of 1m2f/10.6f winner; stable has a good batch of 2yos and this one is respected on his debut.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Lope Y Fernandez; 35,000gns foal, 30,000gns yearling; first foal; dam twice-raced half-sister to useful winners Kings Gift (7f-1m2f including 2yo) and Queens Gift (5f/6f), out of 1m3f winner who stayed 2m; betting likely to provide some good clues on this introduction.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Promise at Bath (firm) on his April debut and again when second at Hamilton (6f, good) in May; wheels came off as favourite next time but that was only one week later on good to soft ground; cheekpieces go on and he could bounce back.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Kameko; fifth foal; closely related to 7f winner Rousham (RPR 67), half-brother to winners First Of May (6f AW including 2yo; 85), Polka (5f AW; 72) and Zachary (5f/6f AW 2yo; 61); dam sprint maiden (55), out of five-time 1m Group 1 winner Soviet Song; interesting to see what the market makes of him on this debut.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50-1 when down the field over 6f at Redcar on last month's introduction; has plenty of winners in her pedigree but only of interest with market support.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: He rallied for third of nine in Doncaster maiden (5f, good to firm) in May and it was a solid effort up at 6f when 25-1 and fifth of 13 in the Woodcote at Epsom (good to soft) on Oaks day; already sets the standard and this new trip promises to unlock more.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to winners Amestris (5f 2yo; RPR 88) and Thisonesforyou (1m 2yo; 64); 25-1 when beaten just over 6l on his introduction here (6f, good) last Monday; worth a glance in the market but likely one for further down the line.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Blackbeard; 21,000euros foal, £35,000 yearling, 50,000euros breeze-up purchase in May; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Up The Anti (5.7f-1m; RPR 64) and Us Carmen (Polish 7f); dam unraced half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Invite, out of 1m2f winner/Oaks third Katiyra; one of the more likely debutants.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 28-1 but she offered plenty to work on when fourth of ten at Leicester (7f, good) a fortnight ago; looked a touch raw on that first go and she's likely to improve with the experience under her belt; considered.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Rajasinghe filly; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Senor Carrots (5f AW; RPR 83) and Great Mates (8.6f AW; 81); dam 5f-1m winner (including 2yo; 94), sister to 6f 2yo winner Lady Katy, out of 5f/6f 2yo winner; yard has only sent out a handful of juveniles this term but is otherwise flying along of late; check the market.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Massaat; second foal; dam poor maiden (RPR 30), sister to French 1m4f winner Finjaans Rose, half-sister to winners Stepney (1m), Lady Charlie (7f 2yo) and Magical Flower (French 6.5f); already gelded and he doesn't feature among the leading newcomers on paper.

Won a tidy six-runner Epsom novice (7f) in September and he built on an encouraging comeback/handicap debut third (also good to soft) last month when going very close at Chester (7.5f, soft) 12 days ago; quicker ground raises a question but he's going the right way and must be factored in.

Four runs here have yielded two seconds and a win in this race in 2024; down the field in last year's renewal but after some modest efforts he's rediscovered his level of late, going close at Hamilton last month and finishing a close third at Ripon three weeks ago; yard going well and he's in the mix again.

Ex-Irish runner who landed a Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) on his November stable debut; subsequently gelded and it was a very creditable fourth in a warm event at Ascot on his handicap debut last month; weakened to finish down the field in another competitive race next time but this represents a drop in grade and he shaped like this stiffer test would suit at Ascot.

Returned to form when 12-1 and prevailing by a nose at Ayr (1m, good to firm) last month; beaten just a neck off this mark at Hamilton last Wednesday and she's likely to play a part again with Tom Marquand taking the reins; due to go up 2lb.
Doubled her tally when winning at Thirsk (1m) in April; has continued to run with credit and it was a very solid fourth of 16 at York (1m1f, also good) 11 days ago; firmly in the picture again.

Sole turf success came at Redcar (1m, good to firm) in August; he is now 16lb higher after a productive spell on AW and finished 12l adrift when returned to turf over C&D last month; down the list despite his yard being in fine form.

Hit the ground running as a juvenile last term but her form tailed off at the backend and she hasn't rediscovered her best in four starts this year; did show a bit more on her last two starts, though, and her mark continues to come down.

Lightly raced 4yo who is yet to score but has shown ability across his six starts; latest third of 11 at Chester (7.5f, soft; acts on quicker) was a respectable effort and it's impossible to say he's not capable of better.

Two AW wins for Hugo Palmer last season (1m, 8.5f); finished last on first two starts for new yard but a switch to blinkers prompted shock return to form when 80-1 and second of seven at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last Monday; interesting to see if the aids work a second time.
Showed a consistent level when placed on all three starts (7f/1m) on a variety of surfaces last year; enters handicaps on his reappearance having been gelded and this opening mark is probably fair.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: She would have gone much closer with a clear run in a handicap at York (7f, good) last month and a return to front-running tactics bore fruit with a Listed win at Epsom (7f, good to soft) latest; below par on sole previous run on fast ground but she has leading form claims under a penalty.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: She's raced nine times and produced her best RPR yet when close second in C&D handicap (good to firm) last month, splitting Albaydaa and Applesandpears who were carrying 2lb more; probably vulnerable back up in grade.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Won at Newbury (7f, good to firm) last June on sole 2yo start and down the field in the Group 3 Fred Darling there (7f, good) on reappearance; beaten only about 3l when sixth of eight in Listed race at Chantilly (1m, good to soft) last month and shaped as though the return to 7f would help; her stable is in excellent form and she might have an each-way shout.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Listed runner-up last October (6f, heavy) and kept on for fourth of 15 in Listed race at Haydock (6f, good to soft) last month, providing optimism that she would be suited by 7f; however, she hasn't seen out 7f either side of that Haydock run, most recently sixth of seven at Musselburgh (good).

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Progressive since handicapping, placed twice in a row (6f/7f, good) before narrow win over this C&D (good to firm); others have stronger form but this well-bred sort could have plenty more left in the tank for her top yard.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: 2-3 in 2yo campaign and returned to form with close third to Albaydaa in C&D handicap (good to firm) last month; they reoppose on the same terms today and this lightly raced contender is open to further improvement, but she has something to find in today's company.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: She's had plenty of racing but has shown improvement on her last two runs, easily winning Class 4 handicap at Lingfield (7f, good to firm) before fifth of sixth to Ellusive Butterfly (who is now penalised) in Epsom Listed race (7f, good to soft), beaten about 4l; on the up and has each-way claims.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: 0-5 in Group/Listed races since winning last July on first two starts but she kept on well for a Listed third at Musselburgh (7f, good) last time and today's stiffer track could be right up her street; one to consider.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Strong form when winning Doncaster maiden (7f, good) last September on second start; disappointing since, including in Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, good) on last month's reappearance, and has something to prove, but she's not written off back down in trip in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Won on debut at Southwell (7f, AW) in April and close second in Redcar novice (6f, good to firm) later that month; she should have a bright future but considerable improvement is needed if she's to threaten today.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Added to his two AW successes since joining these connections when winning over this C&D 25 days ago (good to firm); best form has been on quick ground and he's a contender off this 3lb higher mark; cheekpieces, worn once previously, now return.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Creditable sixth of 16 at York (1m1f, good) before winning at Nottingham (8.5f, good) under Jake Dickson four days ago, making it two wins from his last three starts; escapes a penalty for that success and can't be dismissed.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Promise in three starts for the Gosden stable and he caught the eye when third of 15 at York (1m2f, good) on last month's stable debut; again shaped promisingly at Hamilton (8.5f, good) next time, despite the steady gallop not suiting, but was disappointing back at York 12 days ago, albeit he may have not been helped by ending up towards the far rail; drops in grade and he's the type to bounce back if getting this run to suit.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Won a maiden over 1m at two but all four wins since have been over shorter, the most recent at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) last month; too free at York (7f) 11 days ago and he'll need to settle better now returned to this trip with the hood back on (worn once in 2023).

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
OwnerName: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor
Spotlight: All three wins came in 2024 over 1m2f (good/good to firm) but he was runner-up three times last season; fair effort at York last time and now has first-time blinkers replacing cheekpieces now trying 1m for the first time since his debut.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Hold-up performer whose record on turf reads 1-17 compared to 6-43 on the AW; shaped as if still in form when sixth of 11 at Lingfield (7f, AW) 117 days ago but may need her first outing since.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Six-time winner, the most recent coming on the AW at Southwell off this mark in November; runner-up off a 4lb lower mark in this race in 2024 but he hasn't threatened in three starts this year; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Won four times last year (7f/7.5f) and also posted some other good efforts, including when third of 21 in a competitive handicap at York (7f, good) in October; didn't settle well enough at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in November when last seen but the break may have done her some good.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win since joining this yard came in a first-time visor at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in December and he's ran some good races since the cheekpieces went on; didn't get the best of passages at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) nine days ago and can be treated as if still in form; up in grade but could go well.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner who has won four times since blinkers were added (7f/1m), most recently over 7f at Chester last month off 3lb lower; didn't get home there over 1m2f (good to soft) 11 days ago but should be happier back at this trip.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Made it 2-3 at Musselburgh when winning there over 7f in April off a 1lb lower mark than today's; beaten only 4l when seventh of 19 at York (7f, good) 11 days ago but stamina has to be a concern on just his third try over a 1m.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Heavy-ground maiden winner at Listowel in September when trained by Paddy Twomey; well beaten in the Spring Mile on his yard debut but his two efforts since (1m2f) have been a bit better; cheekpieces now go on but he wouldn't want the ground too lively.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Often front-runner who can race freely; sole win from ten starts came in a Ripon novice (1m, good) in August 2024 but his best effort since undergoing wind surgery came when a 1l third of 11 at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last month; below his best in two starts since.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer (can front run) who won three times last year, twice at Doncaster (1m) and at York (1m, good); reappeared from 203 days off with a solid effort at Newmarket (1m, good) last month but he was withdrawn after becoming upset in the stalls at Doncaster on his last intended start.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Fairly unexposed sort who showed his best form when taking 14-runner Ascot handicap (1m4f, good to firm) seven weeks ago; that race has worked out well, with the third and fourth winning big handicaps at Royal Ascot and at Epsom on Derby day respectively, so a 5lb rise now looks on the lenient side; form of his close second on final start of last year was also boosted at last week's royal meeting and he's a key contender.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 2-2 as a juvenile but he didn't progress at three (quite highly tried) and left Karl Burke for 25,000gns that October; missed last year but there was plenty of encouragement when fourth at Chester (10.5f, good to soft) 11 days ago; fairly quick second run back from a long absence sounds a note of caution but his yard's in fine form; respected.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Dual Flat winner for Harry Eustace (1m2f, good) who also scored twice over hurdles for Jedd O'Keeffe; became very inconsistent for latter yard, however, and he was picked up for £20,000 one month ago; refitted tongue-tie and first-time cheekpieces for this stable debut.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Showed ability in Irish maidens for Noel Meade but yet to get going in two starts for this yard, latterly in a hood (now removed) at Thirsk last month; this is just her third handicap but it's a tough one in which to stage a revival.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Seven-time winner whose three appearances here have yielded two wins (7f and 1m) back in 2022/2023; also finished runner-up in this race (good to firm) in 2024 and he was back on track when fairly close third of four at Hamilton (1m5f, good to firm) four weeks ago; winner and second have won subsequently and he warrants consideration.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Made it win number six when scoring here (1m6f, good; has won at up to 2m2f) last Monday; 5lb penalty makes life tough and he's never won at shorter than 1m6f; evidently in good heart, though.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Two 1m4f wins for Ben Brookhouse on a sound surface but he finished well adrift on his reappearance and first start since wind surgery at Nottingham in April; much more competitive when fifth of 15 at Chester (12.5f, good) one month later; was well drawn that day but it's still early days for this yard.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Not the force of old but, after a couple of modest efforts, his latest fifth of 18 at York (10.5f, good; first-time cheekpieces) was a step back in the right direction; shaped as though this stiffer test might suit on that occasion so interesting to see if the headgear works a second time for in-form yard.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Seemingly exposed gelding who has suddenly got on a roll since going back up in trip, scoring on good ground at Hamilton (1m3f), Musselburgh (12.5f) and over C&D last month; has to defy a further 4lb rise in a better race from the widest stall but he can't be ignored attempting a four-timer.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin
Spotlight: He has been doing well since being gelded and tackling handicaps on a sound surface, winning four-runner event at Leicester (1m4f) and three-runner race at Redcar (1m6f); far from disgraced in a Class 2 at Doncaster last time, bringing this year's form figures to 21213; not fully exposed and likely capable of better yet.

15-race maiden who went very close over C&D just over a year ago; finished down the field in this race (also soft) nine days later but was back in some form from August following wind surgery; reappearance sixth in a blanket finish at Hamilton last month was a solid effort but slow starts have become a theme and she's 2lb 'wrong'.

OwnerName: J Fyffe, A Edmondson & L McGurty
Spotlight: Mostly a hold-up mare; two wins from just five starts here feature a success over this trip (soft) two years ago and another over 6f (good to firm) last May; on her third trainer when scoring over 1m at Newcastle (AW) in January but she'll need to be better than ever to defy this mark on her first start since February; this could set up quite nicely, though.

Got off the mark on her AW reappearance in April and following a solid handicap debut she struck at Wetherby (also 7f, good to firm; made all) three weeks ago; received only a 1lb nudge for that neck success and there's a good chance we've yet to see the best of her.

Took 17 goes to strike for this yard but it all came together at Ripon (1m, good) three weeks ago; will need extra up 3lb but that was the first time she sported this headgear combination and today's stiff 7f is probably not a problem.

Both starts at 7f; encouraging debut when second of five at Haydock (soft) in May and she was away briskly when making all at Yarmouth (good to soft) a fortnight ago; that was a ready win and this better ground ought not to be a concern on pedigree; key contender on her handicap debut.

Went fairly close on her debut over C&D in August and she made all for an easy Catterick maiden (6f, also good) win three weeks later; came up short in Group 3 and Listed company subsequently but cheekpieces go on and she's impossible to rule out ahead of this reappearance/handicap debut; watch the market.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten in third on 5f Dundalk debut; out of her depth in 6f Curragh Group 3 last month; still looked a bit raw when fifth of 17 in a recent 6f maiden at Fairyhouse, but that was an encouraging display nonetheless.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister by Cotai Glory to a 6f/7f winner Kisskodi; dam 7f AW winner, half-sister to 6f Listed winner Castle Hill Cassie.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Failed to sell at 220,000gns at Newmarket last October; fifth foal; half-sister by Blackbeard to winners Awaken (Group-placed 6f 2yo and Alrahy (KSA 1m); dam 1m2f AW winner, half-sister to multiple 1m2f Group 1 winner Twice Over; probably best watched on debut.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Miss Kate Harrington & Mrs John Harrington & B Phelan
Spotlight: Second in a seven-runner maiden over C&D on debut; enough merit in her Curragh fourth (behind the Albany Stakes winner Sun Goddess) to suggest she can go close.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 55,000gns Y (n/s 75,000gns 2yo); first foal; unraced dam closely related to useful winner First Winter, half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Aesop's Fables (Prix Jean Prat), out of useful winning close relative of Derby/Arc winner Lammtarra.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Made an encouraging debut over 5f at Cork last month; failed to build on that in a 6f Fairyhouse race nine days later.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Just touched off in an early-season 5f maiden at Cork; winner Velozee won her next race and has confirmed a fair standard of form in Pattern races won by Victorious, fourth at this venue and eighth in last week's Queen Mary; a leading contender.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fifth foal; Nando Parrado filly is a sister to winners in Spain and Italy; dam placed 1m2f 2yo, half-sister to Group 3 winners Abjer and Gloomy Sunday; fair pedigree but not a likely debut winner in a maiden of this standard.
Sole win came on AW at Newcastle over 6f last winter; hit and miss since and has to rebound from a poor Fairyhouse effort.
Thed last of his nine wins came over 6f at this track last year; has been disappointing in two starts this year but is now 7lb below that last winning mark; needs a return to form.
Finally got off the mark at Navan over this trip last time with cheekpieces replacing other headgear; was a cosy winner then and should go well even off 8lb higher with a visor back on.
Third runner for yard; best form has come over this trip on soft ground, winning at Bellewstown last autumn; fine efforts back there and at Cork this season; drying ground isn't in her favour.
Maiden anticipated the start at Cork last time and was always in rear; can do better but looks the yard's second-string behind Happy Henry.
Best effort came over this trip at Down Royal last year; poor over 7f and 6f both runs this year; has to do much better but coming back to 5f might help.
Generally regressive in the last while and was poor in a Fairyhouse claimer last time in a first-time visor; others make more appeal.
Yet to win but his three best runs have come on AW; not bad runs at Navan over 6f-5f last twice but needs more down 2lb.
Best efforts this year have come over this trip on soft ground, including at Down Royal last time from 7lb out of the handicap; only 2lb 'wrong' today but the drying ground might not suit as well.
All three wins came when trained in UK; has been below form for this yard and has to stage a revival even from a dropping mark.
Maiden has been a close runner-up in three of his last four starts between 5f-6f; an extra 3lb for his Fairyhouse second doesn't help but should be involved again.
Seven wins (incl' at this track) between turf and AW over 5f-6f for former yard; not a bad stable debut at Fairyhouse last time and can be in the mix with some improvement.
Got off the mark at Tipperary last August over this trip; some solid efforts since including on comeback behind Happy Henry at Navan; can improve from that and is in the mix.
Similar efforts in defeat both starts this year; travelled well at Fairyhouse last time over 6f but didn't find a lot off the bridle; coming back to this trip might help.
Dual winner over this trip in 2024; lost his form soon afterwards and has been absent almost a year so is best watched unless market support comes.
Good runs in defeat over this trip at Dundalk and Down Royal lately but was below that level over 6f at Fairyhouse; respected back to 5f.
Thed last of his two wins came at Bellewstown in 2023; some decent runs in defeat this year but was poor at Fairyhouse last time and has to rebound.

OwnerName: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill
Spotlight: Well beaten lately and officially rated only 31 so not easy to fancy.
